title: Dividing a Decimal by a Whole Number

steps.explanation: When the divisor is a whole number, we divide normally and keep the decimal point aligned.

steps.instruction: Divide as if the decimal weren't there, then place it.
steps.explanation: 9.36 has two decimal places, so the answer has two decimal places: 2.34. Check: 4 × 2.34 = 9.36.
Always verify division by multiplying the quotient by the divisor.

title: Dividing When the Divisor Is a Decimal

steps.work: The divisor (0.9) is a decimal, so we need to make it a whole number first.
steps.explanation: Division is easier when the divisor is a whole number.

steps.instruction: Multiply both dividend and divisor by 10.
steps.explanation: Multiplying both numbers by the same power of 10 doesn't change the quotient.
